name: Ourense – Cabeza de Manzaneda – Altos das Malladas
region_intro: Mountain biking around Manzaneda offers diverse terrain within the Serra de Queixa mountain range in eastern Ourense, Spain. The region features vast pine forests covering over 2000 hectares, deep woods with rebollo oak and ancient chestnut trees, and paths shaped by waterways like the Bibei River. Riders can explore high mountain areas and the slopes leading to Cabeza Grande, the highest summit of the Orensano Central Massif. Best mountain bike trails around Manzaneda The most popular mountain bike trail is Ourense – Cabeza de Manzaneda – Altos das Malladas, a 19.2 miles (30.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route leads towards the high points of the Orensano Central Massif. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cabeza de Manzaneda Summit – Cabeza de Manzaneda Summit loop from Manzaneda, a difficult 18.7 miles (30.1 km) path. This loop offers expansive views of the surrounding mountain landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Cabeza de Manzaneda Summit – Alto de Manzaneda loop from Manzaneda, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through high mountain areas, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Mountain biking around Manzaneda is defined by vast pine forests, deep woods, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Manzaneda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 40 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Manzaneda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain bike trails can I expect in Manzaneda?
A: Manzaneda offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from moderate to difficult, winding through vast pine forests, deep rebollo oak and ancient chestnut woods, and along waterways like the Bibei River. The terrain includes high mountain areas and slopes leading to Cabeza Grande, the highest summit of the Orensano Central Massif. You'll find both dedicated bike park trails and extensive marked routes throughout the surrounding natural landscape.

Q: Are there trails suitable for beginners or less experienced mountain bikers?
A: While many routes in Manzaneda are rated as difficult, there are also moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza de Manzaneda Summit – Alto de Manzaneda loop from Manzaneda&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.5-mile (10.4 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 10 minutes to complete, leading through high mountain areas. The Bike Park Manzaneda also features trails of varied technical levels, catering to different rider abilities.

Q: What facilities does the Manzaneda Bike Park offer?
A: The Bike Park Manzaneda is well-equipped for mountain bikers. It features numerous trails of varied technical levels, chairlifts with bike rack systems for easy transport up the mountain, a convenient bike wash service, and showers for riders after their sessions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Manzaneda?
A: Manzaneda is located in a mountainous region, so the best time for mountain biking is typically during the warmer, drier months to avoid snow and ice at higher elevations. The resort operates seasonally, and checking local weather conditions and the Bike Park's operational schedule before your visit is recommended for the best experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the Manzaneda area?
A: Yes, Manzaneda offers several longer routes for those seeking a challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14936772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Bañadoiro Suspension Bridge – Pazo de Arnado loop from O Cruceiro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.6-mile (71.7 km) route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994314812/ourense-cabeza-de-manzaneda-altos-das-malladas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ourense – Cabeza de Manzaneda – Altos das Malladas&lt;/a&gt; trail, which is 19.2 miles (30.9 km) long and leads towards the high points of the Orensano Central Massif.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Manzaneda?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in Manzaneda are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza de Manzaneda Summit – Cabeza de Manzaneda Summit loop from Manzaneda&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 18.7 miles (30.1 km) and offers expansive views of the surrounding mountain landscape.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Manzaneda?
A: The mountain bike routes in Manzaneda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 40 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Manzaneda's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and challenging climbs towards the high points of the Orensano Central Massif.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several notable points of interest. You can find stunning views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza de Manzaneda Summit&lt;/a&gt; itself. Other nearby attractions include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6560180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of Fátima and Fontei Cross&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6560182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel de Montefurado&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass through forests or natural reserves?
A: Manzaneda is set within a vast pine forest covering over 2000 hectares, so many trails naturally wind through these picturesque woods. You'll also find routes that traverse deep rebollo oak forests, gall oaks, and ancient chestnut trees, such as those found in Souto de Rozavales. The region's natural landscape is recognized as a Protected Landscape, highlighting its ecological significance.

Q: What other activities are available in Manzaneda besides mountain biking?
A: Manzaneda is a comprehensive tourist-sports resort offering a wide array of activities. Beyond mountain biking, you can enjoy hiking, mountaineering, climbing, archery, paintball, a go-kart circuit, horse riding, a giant zip line, and a multi-adventure park. The resort also provides amenities such as grass soccer fields, tennis courts, a heated pool, and a spa, making it a versatile destination for outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Manzaneda?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails documented in Manzaneda, ranging in difficulty from moderate to difficult. These routes allow you to explore the diverse landscapes of the Serra de Queixa mountain range.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails with significant elevation changes?
A: Yes, Manzaneda is known for its mountainous terrain and offers trails with substantial elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14936772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Bañadoiro Suspension Bridge – Pazo de Arnado loop from O Cruceiro&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1600 meters of ascent, providing a challenging experience for riders seeking significant climbs.
